QA Internship - Responsibilities and Qualifications Overview
Position Overview:As a QA Intern at our company, you will engage in a comprehensive examination and validation process for our web-based applications and systems. Your role is pivotal in ensuring the functionality, reliability, and performance of our digital products by meticulously evaluating project specifications, testing website features, and identifying areas for enhancement.
Key Responsibilities:
Qualifications:
This internship provides a dynamic environment to develop your professional skills while contributing to the quality assurance of our web applications. You will gain practical experience, work with seasoned professionals, and develop a critical understanding of the technological and user-centric aspects of website development.
- Specification Analysis and Feedback:Carefully review project documentation to fully understand the website functionalities. Provide insightful and constructive feedback to enhance clarity and efficiency.
- Test Case Development:Design detailed, clear, and comprehensive test cases that cover all necessary aspects of website testing to ensure thorough validation of functionality and performance.
- Test Planning:Develop robust test plans that define the scope, approach, and resources required for effective quality assurance of web applications.
- Resource and Time Estimation:Collaborate with the QA team to accurately estimate the time and resources needed to execute test plans efficiently.
- Testing and Prioritization:Execute a variety of tests, including but not limited to functionality, usability, and performance tests. Prioritize testing activities based on project requirements and criticality.
- Issue Documentation:Identify, document, and report any issues or bugs discovered during testing phases. Provide clear and actionable reports to facilitate quick resolutions by the development team.
- User Experience Evaluation:Assess and critique the user interface and user experience to identify potential areas for improvement, aiming to enhance overall user satisfaction.
- Cross-platform Testing:Evaluate the website’s performance, speed, responsiveness, and stability across various platforms, devices, and browsers to ensure consistent user experience.
- Quality Culture Contribution:Actively contribute to the maintenance and enhancement of QA discipline within the team. Promote a culture of quality and continuous improvement throughout the organization.
- Educational Background:Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related technical field is preferred.
- Technical Proficiency:Basic knowledge of web programming languages such as PHP and JavaScript is highly beneficial.
- Experience in Test Automation:Previous experience with test automation tools and performance testing methodologies will be considered an asset.
- Analytical Skills:Demonstrated ability to analyze technical information, identify problems, and propose logical solutions.
- Innovative Thinking:Ability to think creatively and outside the traditional frameworks to develop innovative testing strategies.
- Practical Experience:Prior hands-on experience in website QA, including the development and execution of test cases, planning, and effective reporting.
- Communication Skills:Excellent communication skills, both written and oral, are essential to effectively collaborate with development teams and other stakeholders.

Outright Play is a Sri Lankan music label and entertainment company with one mission:turn artists into extraordinary icons.Our vision is bold:take culture to the world.
Our artists are at the heart of everything we do — but talent alone is not enough. Success requires discipline, organization, and relentless support behind the scenes. The Artist Manager Intern role exists to provide that support: making sure schedules are kept, commitments are followed through, and artists can focus fully on their craft.
This is not a passive internship. It is afull-time, hands-on rolewhere you will work directly with artists, helping manage their day-to-day schedules, communications, and logistics. You will also get exposure to how careers are built — from shows and sponsorships to content and global opportunities.
We are not looking for experienced managers. We are looking fordisciplined, reliable internswho are willing to learn, who take pride in being organized, and who are ready to commit to the demanding but rewarding work of managing talent.
Outright Play is a Sri Lankan music label and entertainment company with one mission:turn artists into extraordinary icons.Our vision is bold:take culture to the world.
We manage artists, produce music, create content, and build brands. From live shows to sponsorships, from music production to branded content, we are raising the standard of entertainment in Sri Lanka to match the world stage.
We are asmall, founder-led teamwith global ambition. That means everyone plays a visible role, and nothing here is “just an internship.” As an Artist Manager Intern, you will be working directly with artists and the founder, gaining rare exposure to what it takes to build and grow a career in music and entertainment.

At Outright Play, you won’t just watch from the sidelines — you’ll be part of the team that makes things happen for our artists.

This role blendscoordination, personal assistance, and hospitality.One moment you may be scheduling rehearsals or following up on brand deliverables; the next you may be arranging food, carrying gear, or ensuring the artist stays hydrated during a performance.

We are not looking for experienced managers. We are looking fordisciplined, reliable, and committed internswho are willing to give the time, care, and focus required to support artists at the highest level.
The Artist Manager Intern supports artists acrossprofessional coordination, personal care, and on-the-ground execution.This means being disciplined, attentive, and available whenever the artist is working — whether it’s a daytime rehearsal, a concert ending at 4 am, or a shoot that wraps at sunrise.

Job_Title: Internship Program 2026 (Interior Design, Marketing, Construction, Operations, Procurement, Customer, Sales)
Job_Description:
Are you highly organized, people-oriented and passionate about great design?Are you excited by bringing creative projects, physical products and spaces to life?
Our internship program may be the right fit for you! It’s a full-time opportunity to work closely with a design-led, home décor and hospitality startup.
We have internship roles spanning design, product development, operations, procurement, construction management, customer support, sales, and marketing. Successful interns may be invited to extend their internship and progress into junior roles within the company.
You will work closely with a highly accomplished team, including successful entrepreneur founders, award-winning architects and designers, and celebrated artists and creatives - an amazing chance to rapidly improve your skills and learn what it takes to bring a successful brand to life.
Read on to find out if this is your next dream role!
Our internships provide a lot of opportunities to craft a career. There are multiple internship roles available, which each include the opportunity to try on many types of workstreams. Interns will take on a broad set of responsibilities, to help them to discover their best strengths and opportunities for careers in the future.
If you have a specific interest in a particular area, please indicate that in your cover letter.
Here are some examples of what interns on our teams can contribute to:
This role offers exposure to working with award-winning architects, artists and creatives: providing a 360º view of bringing an enchanting brand to life.
Working with a startup is a unique experience: there are no heavy layers of management, rigid structures or “that’s just the way it’s always been done”. We are often venturing into new areas, unfamiliar industries and developing new skillsets.
1. Impactful Work:All of our team members take on important projects, interns included. You'll have a chance to have a real impact for our company and partners.That also means:it's very important that work is done on time and to a high standard.
2. Flexibility:There are no overbearing managers or micromanagement. You will have guidance and support but no micromanagement.That also means:You will be responsible for your own outcomes. You will need to actively manage your time, and actively solicit feedback on your work to ensure that what you deliver is on schedule and provides value.
3. Full Participation:Good ideas come from anyone, including you. We care about the quality of the idea, work and always improving outcomes for us and our partners.That also means:you'll be expected to actively contribute ideas and participate in feedback for other's work outside of your chosen project.
Much of this work happens behind the scenes, and it needs to be done thoughtfully and well.
As part of your onboarding, you'll work with a senior member of the team to build a 90-day plan. This will help you to succeed in your initial contract period!
These are paid internships. Our interns take on real work, and are compensated accordingly and competitively.
These are full-time internships (40 hours per week, M-F), offering a flexible and dynamic working setup.
Please note any restrictions on your schedule in your application.We aim to accommodate where possible!
For a candidate to succeed in this role, you will need the following:
This role requires being present, engaged, and comfortable taking initiative.
Please highlight any work or project experience and clearly explain how it applies.
In your resume or cover letter, feel free to highlight any projects, work, or experiences that show how you might contribute across the types of work this role involves.
This can include internships, part-time work, volunteer roles, school or club projects, creative side projects, or personal initiatives.
You do not need formal work experience in these areas to apply. If you’re motivated, organized, and eager to learn, we encourage you to apply and tell us what you’d bring to the team.
Apply through the Rooster platform. Please include a short note or letter (~100 words is fine) with your application. Let us know:
Why are you interested in working for Kohomba? How does it tie into your future career goals?
If you are someone who enjoys building community, ambitious projects, and is looking to join a team with high standards who constantly pushes to improve and grow, we look forward to your application!
Come embark on an exciting journey in craft with us!
Applications without an attached note or letter will not be considered.
- Event Operations:Supporting day-to-day operations of our co-working studio space, including facilitating bookings, welcoming guests into the space and helping them get set up.
- Customer Relationship Management:Responding to customer inquiries and supporting a high-quality customer experience.
- Interior Design & Construction Site Coordination:Supporting our lead interior designer and architect by coordinating with contractors, construction teams, and vendors; assisting with site logistics, deliveries, schedules, and ensuring design plans are both communicated and executed accurately.
- Marketing:Supporting marketing coordination, including working with our different agencies and freelancers on website updates, graphic assets, and campaign materials.
- Content Creation:Assisting with content creation, including written or video content. Developing your skills in social media marketing, SEO and digital marketing fundamentals.
- Creative Shoots:Helping prepare spaces, materials, and logistics for creative shoots, including coordinating and working with agencies, photographers, and internal teams.
- Logistics & Supply Chain:Coordinating shipments and supporting basic logistics. Helping with packing and procurement where necessary.
